title: T-077 — Manufacturing Operations List screen (SET-055)
prompt:
# T-077 — Manufacturing Operations List screen (SET-055)

## Goal
Build SET-055 spec-driven from `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` §8.9 / UX SET-055 plus the production contract notes below. The adjacent Reference Data prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/admin-screens.jsx:475-535` is pattern-only reference for table/card density and primitives, not a 1:1 screen source.

Operations data grid. Reorder via drag-to-reorder (auto-save per drag → T-038 reorderOperations). Filter by industry_code + show-inactive toggle. Bulk action: 'Reset to seed data'.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/manufacturing-operations/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/manufacturing-operations/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the production component renders, then it satisfies UX SET-055 Manufacturing Operations List / PRD §8.9.4 as the source of truth. Adjacent prototype snippets may be reused only as pattern references for matching primitives/density; they must not override UX route, fields, states, validation, or Manufacturing Operations semantics.
- Given industry='bakery' filter is applied, when page renders, then only bakery rows are listed.
- Given row drag-to-reorder fires, when handler runs, then T-038 reorderOperations is called with the new (id, operation_seq) array.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Add a failing test that exercises the acceptance criteria above. The RED command is:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/reference/manufacturing-operations/page.test.tsx [create]`
2. Run RED — it must fail before any production code is written.
3. Implement the smallest change needed for GREEN.
4. Re-run RED command — must pass.
5. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not edit files outside the listed scope without documenting why in CLOSEOUT.
- Do not weaken existing acceptance criteria from prior tasks.
- Do not introduce REST endpoints; use Server Actions per stack contract.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED→GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC: pass/fail)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. Primitives in `packages/ui`. PRD: `docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md` v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.description: SET-055 Manufacturing Operations List production component (spec-driven; no exact prototype).
pipeline_inputs.details: Spec-driven UI: no exact canonical prototype exists under `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/*.jsx` for SET-055 Manufacturing Operations List. `prototypes/design/02-SETTINGS-UX.md` §8.9 and PRD §8.9.4 replace prototype parity as the authoritative contract. prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/admin-screens.jsx:475-535 is adjacent/pattern-only reference for matching primitives, density, and interaction conventions; it is not a 1:1 source and must not change Manufacturing Operations fields, states, validation, route, or semantics. Production contract: data via Server Components (Drizzle); mutations via Server Actions; primitives from `packages/ui` only (no @radix-ui/react-dialog import outside packages/ui per §0).
acceptance_criteria: Given the production component renders, then it satisfies UX SET-055 Manufacturing Operations List / PRD §8.9.4 as the source of truth. Adjacent prototype snippets may be reused only as pattern references for matching primitives/density; they must not override UX route, fields, states, validation, or Manufacturing Operations semantics.
Given industry='bakery' filter is applied, when page renders, then only bakery rows are listed.
Given row drag-to-reorder fires, when handler runs, then T-038 reorderOperations is called with the new (id, operation_seq) array.

UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: write RTL test that mounts the page with mocked data and asserts the parity AC items + ac_extra coverage.
GREEN: build component, wire Server Action(s).
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not import @radix-ui/react-dialog outside packages/ui (§0 ESLint rule).
Do not paste prototype JSX wholesale or treat adjacent prototypes as 1:1 sources — translate UX §8.9 to the production contract.
Do not regress accessibility (axe violations = fail).
Do not claim 1:1 prototype parity for this screen; UX §8.9 is authoritative.
pipeline_inputs.prototype_index_entry: manufacturing_ops_screen (ManufacturingOpsScreen list view — shared file with SET-056 edit modal)
